overview: The witest28-9 system is a minimal single-component architecture consisting of one frontend web application that serves a static "Hello, World!" page directly to the browser. The web-app is a server-side rendered Go (net/http) service running on port 9090, delivering pre-rendered HTML with embedded CSS — no backend API or database is required. All traffic is routed through the platform's ingress layer which terminates HTTPS before reaching the container.
requirements: The root URL path (/) must return HTTP 200 with a valid HTML page displaying 'Hello, World!' as the primary heading.
The browser tab title must be set to 'Hello World' via a <title> tag in <head>.
Navigating to any undefined route must return an HTTP 404 response with a user-friendly 'Not Found' message.
The page must be responsive and render correctly on screen widths from 320px to 2560px.
Text must meet a minimum contrast ratio of 4.5:1 against the background (WCAG AA compliance).
The <html> element must include a lang attribute (e.g., lang='en').
All meaningful content must be accessible to screen readers.
The page must fully load in under 2 seconds on a 25 Mbps connection.
Total page weight (HTML + CSS + assets) must not exceed 500 KB.
The application must handle at least 100 concurrent users without errors or degraded performance.
Response time must be under 500 ms under normal load.
The application must not expose sensitive server version information in HTTP response headers (e.g., Server header must be suppressed or genericised).
The application must not collect, store, or transmit any user data.
A /health endpoint must return HTTP 200 to support liveness/readiness probes.
The application must achieve at least 99% monthly uptime.

components.name: hello-world-web
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Hello World Web App
  description: Serves the Hello World HTML page and health endpoint.
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /:
    get:
      summary: Hello World page
      description: Returns the main Hello World HTML page.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: HTML page with Hello World greeting.
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      description: Returns 200 OK when the service is healthy.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /{path}:
    get:
      summary: Catch-all 404
      description: Returns a user-friendly 404 Not Found page for undefined routes.
      parameters:
        - name: path
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '404':
          description: Page not found.
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
components.componentAgentInstructions:
## Component: hello-world-web ### Overview Implement a lightweight Go web server using the standard `net/http` package. The server listens on **port 9090** and serves a pre-rendered HTML page with a 'Hello, World!' greeting. There is no database, no external API calls, and no user data collection. ### Repository layout (within /hello-world-web) ``` /hello-world-web Dockerfile go.mod main.go ``` ### go.mod - Module name: `github.com/witest28-9/hello-world-web` - Go version: 1.22 - No external dependencies — standard library only. ### main.go — Key behaviours 1. **HTTP Mux**: Use `http.NewServeMux()`. 2. **Routes**: - `GET /` → serve the Hello World HTML (inline string constant or embedded file). Must return HTTP 200 and `Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8`. - `GET /health` → return HTTP 200 JSON `{"status":"ok"}` with `Content-Type: application/json`. - Default (catch-all) → return HTTP 404 with a user-friendly HTML page containing a 'Page Not Found' message and a link back to `/`. Must return `Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8`. 3. **Security headers** — set on every response via a middleware wrapper: - `Server: ` (empty string, to suppress Go's default) - `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff` - `X-Frame-Options: DENY` - `Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; style-src 'unsafe-inline'` - `Referrer-Policy: no-referrer` 4. **Graceful shutdown**: On SIGINT/SIGTERM, call `server.Shutdown(ctx)` with a 10-second timeout. 5. **Logging**: Use `log.Printf` to log each request (method, path, status code, duration). Do NOT log any client IP addresses or user-identifying information. ### HTML page (inline constant `indexHTML`) ```html Hello World

Hello, World!

``` ### 404 HTML page (inline constant `notFoundHTML`) ```html 404 – Not Found

404 – Page Not Found

Return to Home

``` ### Dockerfile ```dockerfile # Build stage FROM golang:1.22-alpine AS builder WORKDIR /app COPY go.mod . RUN go mod download COPY . . RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build -ldflags='-s -w' -o server ./main.go # Runtime stage FROM scratch COPY --from=builder /app/server /server EXPOSE 9090 ENTRYPOINT ["/server"] ``` ### Performance & reliability requirements to satisfy - All response bodies must be pre-rendered strings (no templating at request time) so latency stays well under 500 ms. - The `scratch` base image keeps the image tiny (< 10 MB) and total page weight well under 500 KB. - The server must start within 1 second to support fast rolling restarts. ### Edge cases - A request to `/health` must never return a non-200 even during high load — it has no dependencies. - The mux must match `/` exactly; `GET /foo` must fall through to the 404 handler, not the index handler. - Do not use `http.FileServer` — serve HTML from in-memory string constants only.
